description_long: This is a traditional Japanese samurai kabuto helmet, featuring a domed black iron bowl with prominent rivets and a pair of large, gold-colored crests (maedate) at the front. The helmet is adorned with wide, flaring neck guards (shikoro) made of overlapping red lacquered plates, each bordered with gold rivets and blue lacing. The side flaps (fukigaeshi) are decorated with metallic and patterned embellishments, adding both protection and ornate detail. The front displays a circular decorative boss at the center, framed by the gold crest. The lower face area is protected by a black menpo (face guard) with a fierce, stylized mouth and chin. The overall construction combines metal, lacquer, and textile elements, reflecting both functionality and ceremonial artistry.
description: A traditional Japanese samurai kabuto helmet with a black domed bowl, gold crest, red lacquered neck guard, and ornate side flaps. Features a decorative boss and a black menpo face guard.
description_view_0: Front view shows the gold crest, central boss, black face guard, and symmetrical side flaps.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the layered red neck guard, side flap details, and the profile of the gold crest.
description_view_2: Rear view emphasizes the wide, flared red neck guard and the back of the domed black bowl.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the red neck guard, side flap, and gold crest from the opposite angle.
attribute_graphic_design: The helmet features a traditional Japanese design with gold crests, patterned side flaps, and blue lacing on red lacquered plates. No modern graphics or text are present.
